title: MinION DNA Library Prep (SQK-LSK109)

steps: Make up 1 µg of DNA sample in nuclease-free water to a volume of 49 µL in an Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Make 150 µL of fresh 70% ethanol. Place an 1.5 mL tube of nuclease-free water on a heat block set to 55 °C .
Resuspend the Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880 by vortexing. Add 150 µL, AMPure beads to the DNA and gently flick the tube for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack and wait for the beads to pellet on the magnet. Once the beads have pelleted, pipette off and discard the supernatant.
Without disturbing the pellet on the magnet, wash the beads with 50 µL, 70% ethanol . Pipette off the ethanol and discard.
Allow the bead pellet to dry on the magnet with the tube's lid open for about 00:00:30 , but do not allow the pellet to dry out completely.

Remove the tube from the magnet. Elute the DNA with 50 µL, nuclease-free water at 55 °C . Gently flick the tube for 00:05:00 to resuspend the beads and replace the tube on the magnet. Once the beads have pelleted, pipette off and retain 50 µL of the eluate. Discard the beads.
Follow the Qubit DNA HS Assay protocol to quantify 1 µL of clean DNA from the clean-up step.

Thaw DNA CS at Room temperature , mix by pipetting and place On ice . Thaw NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix - 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #M6630S and NEBNext® Ultra™ II End Repair/dA-Tailing Module New England Biolabs Catalog #E7546 On ice .

In a 0.2 mL PCR tube, mix: 1 µL, DNA CS 47 µL, DNA sample 3.5 µL, NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Buffer 2 µL, NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix 3.5 µL, Ultra II End-prep Reaction Buffer 3 µL, Ultra II End-prep Enzyme Mix
Mix by gently flicking the tube, then spin down. Incubate at 20 °C for 00:20:00 , then at 65 °C for 00:05:00 using a thermocycler.
Resuspend the Agencourt AmPure XP beads Contributed by users Catalog #A63880 by vortexing. Transfer the DNA mixture from the PCR tube to an Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 60 µL, AMPure beads to the tube and flick the tube to mix.
Incubate on a rotator mixer or invert the tube by hand for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Meanwhile, prepare 500 µL, 70% ethanol using nuclease-free water.
After the incubation, spin down the sample using a microfuge and place on the magnetic rack. Once a pellet has formed, pipette off the supernatant, leaving tube on the magnet.
With the tube still on the magnet, wash the beads without disturbing the pellet with 200 µL, 70% ethanol . Pipette off the ethanol and discard.
Spin down the tube using a microfuge and replace on the magnet - pipette off residual ethanol and leave the lid open to allow the pellet to dry for around 00:00:30 .
Remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the pellet in 61 µL, nuclease-free water heated to 55 °C . Incubate for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Replace the tube on the magnet to pellet the beads. Remove and retain 61 µL of the eluate in an Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Follow the Qubit DNA HS Assay protocol to quantify 1 µL of DNA from the previous step.

Spin down AMX and NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase. Place On ice . Thaw LNB at Room temperature , spin down and mix by pipetting. Place On ice . Thaw EB at Room temperature , vortex to mix and place On ice . Thaw LFB or SFB at Room temperature , vortex to mix and place On ice .

In an Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, mix: 60 µL, DNA sample 25 µL, LNB 10 µL, NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 5 µL, AMX Gently flick the tube to mix, then spin down. Incubate the tube at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Vortex the AMPure beads to resuspend. Add 40 µL, AMPure beads to the reaction mix from the previous step. Gently flick the tube to mix.
Incubate the tube on a rotator mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Spin down using a microfuge and place the tube on the magnetic rack. Once a pellet has formed, pipette off and discard the supernatant.

Add 250 µL, LFB or 250 µL, SFB to the tube. Gently flick the tube to resuspend the beads. Spin the tube down briefly, then replace on the magnetic rack. Once a pellet has formed, pipette off the supernatant and discard.
go to step #27
Using a microfuge, spin down the tube and replace on the magnet. Pipette off any residual supernatant and discard.
Allow the pellet to dry with the lid open for about 00:00:30 .
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack. Add 15 µL, EB and gently flick the tube to resuspend the pellet. Incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C .
Spin down very briefly using a microfuge and replace the tube on the magnet. Once a pellet has formed slowly pipette off and retain 15 µL of the eluate and place On ice .
Follow the Qubit DNA HS Assay protocol to quantify 1 µL of the DNA library from the previous step.
go to step #4
go to step #16
Thaw SQB, LB, FLT and a fresh tube of FB at Room temperature . Once thawed, place FB On ice . Mix SQB, FB and FLT by vortexing, spin down using a microfuge and place On ice .
Open the lid of the MinION. Slide the MinION SpotON flow cell under the MinION clip.

Rotate open the priming port cover. Place a P1000 pipette tip (pipette set to 200 μL) upright in the priming port. Slowly turn the volume dial to ~220 μL. There should be a small volume of yellow buffer in the pipette tip. Still holding the pipette in an upright position, swiftly and smoothly lift the pipette out of the priming port.
Add 30 µL, FLT directly to a fresh tube of FB to make the flow cell priming mix. Vortex to mix.

Load 800 µL of the priming mix into the priming port: Hold the pipette upright in the priming port and turn the dial to gradually to force the priming mix into the flow cell. (To avoid the introduction of air, you may need to turn the dial a small amount in the opposite direction to draw up some of the flow cell buffer and establish liquid-to-liquid contact between the buffer and priming mix.) Wait for 00:05:00 .
Thoroughly mix LB to resuspend the beads by pipetting.
In an Eppendorf LoBind tube, mix: 37.5 µL, SQB 25.5 µL, LB (freshly mixed) 12 µL, DNA library
Lift the SpotON sample port cover and load an additional 200 µL of the priming mix into the still open priming port .
Mix the loading library by slowly pipetting to resuspend the loading beads.
Slowly, drop 75 µL loading library into the SpotON sample port. Make sure the priming port is still open during this step and the library should easily fall into the sample port.
Close the priming port and replace the SpotON sample port cover. Close the lid of the MinION device. Start the sequencing experiment on the MinKNOW software.
Check the number of pores on the flow cell by navigating to and selecting Flow Cell Check on the MinKNOW software. This should take approximately 00:10:00 .
creator: Victoria LN Jackson
